broker_message: Hi Mikhail — interested in 1500 Ocean Parkway #4G. It looks like it's been relisted a few times since last summer — can you tell me why it hasn't rented? Also: does it need co-op board approval (fees/timeline), which way does 4G face, is it rent-stabilized with the legal rent, and full move-in costs? Given the time on market, would the owner consider $1,850? Thanks.
broker_questions: Why relisted several times since August 2025?
Does renting need co-op board approval — fees/timeline?
Is it rent-stabilized, and the legal rent?
Full move-in cost and lease term?
Is heat/hot water included and reliable?
Are laundry and elevator in service?
brokerage: Apartment Rentals and Sales Realty LLC
building_risk_rationale: openigloo shows just 1 open violation (0/unit vs 0.81 avg), no evictions, no bedbugs, 0 Class C — the cleanest building of the group. 3 litigation cases (typical for a 72-unit co-op). Owner Chaim Babad runs ~40 buildings with a mixed portfolio-wide reputation (~75 open violations across the portfolio), so building-level cleanliness here is reassuring. Low risk for this specific building.
commute_notes: Nearest is the F (Culver) at Avenue N/P, ~7-8 min. F to Midtown is slow (~50-60 min). Faster B/Q at Kings Highway is farther (~13 min walk).
cons: Closest train is the slow F; faster B/Q is a ~13-min walk
Co-op rental may require board approval, fees, and sublet restrictions
Relisted repeatedly since Aug 2025 — find out why
leverage_notes: Dropped from $1,995 to $1,950 and failed to rent across two brokerages since Aug 2025 — clear leverage. Comps $1,875-$2,025, so $1,950 is fair but churn justifies pushing to ~$1,850 plus waived app fees.
most_likely_sitting_reason: Priced slightly high for a slow-F-train Ocean Parkway 1BR and possibly slowed by co-op board/sublet friction; the cut and churn point to price/terms, not a defect
noise_risks: Ocean Parkway is a wide multi-lane arterial — traffic noise on the avenue side
plain_english: A no-fee real 1-bedroom in a notably clean, well-run Midwood co-op (only 1 open violation, no evictions/bedbugs) with elevator, laundry, live-in super — the safest building of the group. Trade-offs: nearest subway is the slow F (faster B/Q is a longer walk), and as a co-op rental it may need board approval and fees. It has bounced on/off market since last summer with a small cut — your opening to negotiate toward ~$1,850 and waived app fees. Worth touring; ask why it hasn't rented and clarify the co-op process.
pros: Cleanest building of the group — 1 open violation, no evictions, no bedbugs
No fee, elevator + laundry + live-in super, real 1-bedroom
Relist churn gives genuine negotiating leverage toward ~$1,850

red_flags.how_to_verify: Ask why it didn't rent before
red_flags.why: Listed 8/2025 at $1,950, relisted 12/2025 at $1,995, now $1,950 by a new brokerage — struggled to rent over ~10 months

red_flags.how_to_verify: Tour at a busy time; ask which way 4G faces
red_flags.why: Busy arterial; facing unit may get traffic noise

red_flags.issue: No sqft/floor plan despite 'Large 1 Bed' claim

red_flags.how_to_verify: Ask about board approval, fees, sublet term limits
red_flags.issue: Co-op rental — board approval/sublet rules may apply
red_flags.why: Board application, fees, and sublet limits can add time/cost

unverified: Whether unit requires co-op board approval and fees
Exact move-in costs and lease term
Why the unit has repeatedly failed to rent since Aug 2025
